skill.name: brand-landingpage
skill.description: Brand-first landing page designer — runs a brand-identity interview (colors, typography, shape language), then generates and iterates on a polished landing page via Stitch with deployment-ready HTML. Use when the user asks to create, design, or build a landing page, homepage, or marketing page and has no established visual direction. Skip when they have a design mockup, need a dashboard or app UI, are working at component level, building a multi-page app, or restyling with known design tokens — use frontend-design instead.
skill.summary: Brand-first landing page designer that generates polished pages through brand interviews and Stitch SDK.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: All 295 static analysis findings were evaluated against actual file content. All 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives caused by hex color codes in brand design reference tables. All 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from markdown code blocks showing SDK method names and CLI commands. The 'C2 keywords', 'system reconnaissance', 'Windows SAM database', and 'Windows cmd.exe' findings are all false positives resulting from design terminology, brand interview questions, and legitimate file-opening instructions. The 'environment file access' finding is confirmed but represents proper API key management with documented security practices. The 'hardcoded URL' findings are low-severity documentation links for the Stitch SDK. The heuristic 'dangerous combination' finding is a false positive reflecting expected SDK integration patterns (API key + SDK calls + documentation references). No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains hardcoded URLs pointing to Stitch SDK documentation. These are legitimate documentation links but represent external network references in the skill instructions.

content.user_title: Design a Brand-First Landing Page with Interactive Interviews
content.value_statement: Most landing pages fail because they skip the brand identity step. This skill guides users through a structured brand interview, then generates a polished landing page with a matching design system using the Stitch SDK.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through a structured brand identity interview covering product purpose, brand feel, and visual preferences
Generates a complete design system with fonts, colors, and shapes mapped from interview answers
Creates and iterates on landing page designs using the Stitch SDK with desktop and mobile variants
Saves HTML output locally and opens designs in the browser for high-fidelity visual review
Manages session state with automatic resume support and metadata persistence
Delivers a deployment-ready bundle with HTML, design tokens, and deployment checklist
limitations: Cannot accept image uploads or embed user-provided logos directly into the generated page
Does not support animations, interactivity, or dynamic content in generated pages
Limited to single-page landing sites and marketing pages, not multi-page applications or dashboards
Requires a working Stitch SDK setup and API key for design generation

use_cases.title: Launch a Side Project Landing Page
use_cases.description: A developer building a CLI tool or open source project needs a landing page but has no design experience. This skill runs a brand interview and generates a professional page.

use_cases.title: Create a Startup Marketing Site
use_cases.description: An early-stage startup needs a landing page for their SaaS product. The skill helps define brand identity and produces a page with features, pricing, and testimonials sections.

use_cases.title: Build a Developer Portfolio Page
use_cases.description: A developer or designer wants a personal portfolio landing page. The skill conducts a brand interview and generates a page with featured projects and about sections.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Landing Page Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page for my project called [Project Name]. It is a [description]. The target users are [audience].
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a basic landing page request

prompt_templates.title: Full Brand Interview Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to design a landing page for my product [Project Name]. Let me go through the brand interview to define my visual identity first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a complete brand identity from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Design Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: The hero section on my landing page feels too plain. Can you generate a variant with a bolder layout and more color contrast? Keep the color scheme.

prompt_templates.title: Resume a Saved Session
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a saved design session from yesterday. Resume from where I left off and help me finalize the bundle for deployment.

output_examples.input: Create a landing page for my API tool called DataPipe
output_examples.output: The skill asks about your product purpose, then guides you through choosing 3 brand adjectives like clean, technical, and modern. After confirming a dark theme with indigo accents, it generates a desktop landing page with hero, code snippet, features, and footer sections.

output_examples.input: The colors feel wrong, can you change them?
output_examples.output: The skill asks what feels off about the colors: too bright, too dull, or the wrong hue. After you say too bright, it generates a muted variant with the same layout but adjusted color saturation.

output_examples.input: I want to compare different layout options
output_examples.output: The skill generates 3 variants of the landing page with a centered hero, a split layout, and a full-width header. It opens all three in browser tabs and asks which direction you prefer.

best_practices: Complete the brand interview even if you want to skip it. The questions take 5 minutes and make the difference between a generic template and a page that fits your product.
Review generated pages by opening the HTML in your browser rather than asking for text descriptions. Visual feedback leads to better design iterations.
Save your Stitch API key in your environment before starting so the skill can generate and iterate on designs without interruption.
anti_patterns: Asking the skill to embed your company logo or upload images. The Stitch SDK generates from text prompts and does not accept image inputs.
Providing CSS-level feedback like adding padding-top 40px. Translate feedback into design intent such as add more breathing room above the headline.
Skipping the mobile variant. The desktop layout often needs adjustments for mobile viewing and generating both ensures a consistent experience.

faq.question: Do I need a Stitch account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses the Stitch SDK to generate landing page designs. You need a Stitch account and API key.

faq.question: What happens if my session is interrupted?
faq.answer: The skill saves progress to a metadata.json file. When you return, it checks the saved state and resumes from where you left off.

faq.question: Can I use my own brand colors?
faq.answer: Yes. During the brand interview you can provide existing hex values for your brand colors. The skill maps them to the design system.

faq.question: Does this skill work with images and logos?
faq.answer: No. The Stitch SDK generates pages from text prompts only. User-provided images are saved to the output bundle for manual insertion.

faq.question: What kind of pages can this skill create?
faq.answer: Single-purpose landing pages and product marketing sites. Not multi-page applications, dashboards, or documentation sites.

faq.question: Can I edit the HTML after generation?
faq.answer: The skill recommends iterating through the Stitch SDK first. After approval, you can hand-edit the final HTML output.
